title: The One With Phoebes Rats

jokes.text: She's at home putting up decorations... I tried, but apparently singing 'I Will Survive' in a helium voice, not helping.
jokes.setup: Monica is decorating for Rachel's party and Chandler offers to help
jokes.punchline: His 'help' was singing 'I Will Survive' in helium voice - completely unhelpful
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - well-structured joke with specific absurd visual and good character voice

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'll help you decorate!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really? That would be great.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I Will Survive! I Will Survive! Hey hey!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: ...What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm helping! In helium voice!

jokes.text: Don't say that loud. Gunther's gonna want to hug me.
jokes.setup: Rachel enters the coffee shop
jokes.punchline: She's afraid speaking loudly will attract unwanted attention from Gunther
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character dynamic, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't speak loudly in here.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Gunther's been staring at me all morning.

jokes.text: Somebody's getting a little fussy. You're damn right I am. I've been waiting for a cookie for seven minutes.
jokes.setup: Ross comments that 'somebody's getting fussy' about the baby
jokes.punchline: Chandler thinks Ross is talking about him waiting for a cookie
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - audience assumes baby talk, Chandler's wounded pride about cookie service is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Somebody's getting fussy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm not fussy. I'm just... I've been waiting for that cookie for twenty minutes.

jokes.text: If I wasn't married, she'd be rejecting me right now.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether the nanny is hot
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware admission that he'd definitely be rejected
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - self-deprecating with confident delivery, very quotable structure

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, she's not that hot. I mean, if she were interested, I'd have no shot anyway. I'd probably say something stupid like, 'Did it hurt?' and she'd be like, 'Did what hurt?' and I'd say, 'When you fell from heaven?' and she'd just stare at me in horror.

jokes.text: Now that Rachel's gone? So hot, I cried myself to sleep last night.
jokes.setup: Ross initially downplays nanny's attractiveness when Rachel is present
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits intense attraction and pathetic behavior once Rachel leaves
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Ross's dishonesty and patheticness - the crying detail is perfectly Ross

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She's not even that pretty.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, I mean, she's okay, but nothing special.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: [leaves]

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God, she is so hot! I'm pathetic!

jokes.text: It starts with a V and ends with an X. And hopefully with a T-O in the middle. You know, come to think of it, the capital of Peru is 'Vtox.'
jokes.setup: Mike correctly says Lima is capital of Peru, Phoebe insists it's wrong
jokes.punchline: She creates nonsense word 'Vtox' incorporating detox reference
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe-esque absurd logic, though setup is a bit convoluted

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, no. The capital of Peru is Vtox.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Phoebe, Lima is the capital of Peru.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, maybe where you're from. In Peru, it's Vtox. It's like detox, but with a V.

jokes.text: Well, not so much a pet as, you know, an occasional visitor who I put food out for. Kind of like Santa.
jokes.setup: Mike asks if Bob is her pet rat
jokes.punchline: Phoebe compares feeding a rat to leaving cookies for Santa
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Phoebe logic with unexpected but charming comparison

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Is Bob your pet rat?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No! Well, I mean, he is a rat, but he's not a pet. He's more like... a roommate who doesn't pay rent. It's like leaving cookies out for Santa, except the rat actually shows up.

jokes.text: Except Santa doesn't poop on the plate of cookies.
jokes.setup: Phoebe just compared rat to Santa
jokes.punchline: Immediate acknowledgment of the key difference - Santa's bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with gross-out punchline, very economical

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, but Santa doesn't poop in your apartment.

jokes.text: I don't know, but they don't sound like spa treatments.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks what leptospirosis and hantavirus are
jokes.punchline: Mike's dry observation that diseases don't sound relaxing
jokes.explanation: Solid deadpan delivery, good contrast setup but not particularly memorable

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What are leptospirosis and hantavirus?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: They're diseases. And they don't sound very relaxing.

jokes.text: It's so weird you think he's so gross, yet you're willing to eat his crackers.
jokes.setup: Mike wants Phoebe to get rid of the rat
jokes.punchline: Phoebe points out Mike eats food the rat has been near
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with Phoebe's typical contrarian logic

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: You have to get rid of that rat.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: But Mike, you eat food that the rat has been near.

jokes.text: Is this your first apology?
jokes.setup: Rachel gives backhanded apology calling Gavin pompous, arrogant, obnoxious
jokes.punchline: Gavin sarcastically questions her apology technique
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that highlights Rachel's terrible apology skills

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight. You're apologizing... by insulting me?

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: That's quite an apology technique you've got there.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Obviously, Heather's ass has something more important to say, so I'll just wait till it's finished.
jokes.setup: Rachel is apologizing to Gavin but he's looking at another woman
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically personifies the woman's butt as interrupting their conversation
jokes.explanation: Clever personification joke with good sarcastic edge, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry, Gavin. I really want to talk to you about this.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure, no problem.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, excuse me! We're trying to have a conversation here!

jokes.text: Or was it pants? I didn't really see what happened below the ass area.
jokes.setup: Gavin claims he was looking at her skirt professionally
jokes.punchline: Admits he was focused on her butt and didn't notice anything else
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gavin's shamelessness, though predictable

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: I was looking at your skirt professionally.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: I was focused on your butt and didn't notice anything else.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. I'm jealous. 'Oh, Gavin. Please, please look at my ass.'
jokes.setup: Gavin asks if Rachel is jealous
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically mimics begging for his attention
jokes.explanation: Functional sarcasm but fairly predictable and not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Are you jealous?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes! Please, please, please pay attention to me! Look at me!

jokes.text: One: I didn't know that you knew that. And two: I wasn't some creep staring at his ass.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to explain why her relationship with Tag was different
jokes.punchline: First reason is just admitting ignorance, second undermines her point
jokes.explanation: Good escalating logic failure showing Rachel's weak defense

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, it was different because... I don't know, it just was!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And second of all, he's really, really hot.

jokes.text: It was just Tag, you know, like Cher or... you know, Moses.
jokes.setup: Rachel can't remember Tag's last name
jokes.punchline: Compares him to famous single-name figures, including Moses
jokes.explanation: Clever comparison with Moses being unexpectedly funny addition to the list

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: His last name is... I don't know. Wait, who needs a last name? Moses didn't have a last name. Just Moses. Madonna, just Madonna. Cher, just Cher.

jokes.text: What are you, his boyfriend?
jokes.setup: Gavin corrects her that Tag's last name was Jones, not Morgan
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically questions why he knows so much about Tag
jokes.explanation: Generic comeback, relies on lazy gay joke without any cleverness

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why do you know so much about Tag?

jokes.text: I wasn't staring. I was leering.
jokes.setup: Ross accuses Chandler of staring at the nanny
jokes.punchline: Chandler 'corrects' him with an even worse behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - economy of language with escalating admission, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I wasn't staring at the nanny!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, you were!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. But in my defense, she does have a great personality.

jokes.text: Yeah, obvious beauty's the worst. You know, when it's right there in your face. Me, I like to have to work to find someone attractive. Makes me feel like I earned it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of nanny being obviously attractive
jokes.punchline: Ross's absurd philosophy that he prefers having to work to find attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Ross logic - the idea of 'earning' attraction is perfectly neurotic

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I prefer a woman where I have to work to find the attractiveness. You know, like, she's got a great personality, and then one day you look at her and you go, 'Wow, I can see how others might find her attractive.'

jokes.text: Hey, that was nice of you guys to back off and let Joey get the girl for once.
jokes.setup: Joey appears to be doing well with the nanny
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically suggests they deliberately let Joey win
jokes.explanation: Decent character banter but not particularly memorable or clever

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, that's a great idea. Let's just let Joey win so he feels better about himself.

jokes.text: They've elected me to talk to you about the baby talk. It's not so good.
jokes.setup: Chandler approaches Ross about his baby talk to Emma
jokes.punchline: Delivers critique in characteristically understated way
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler delivery with diplomatic understatement of the problem

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Dude, you gotta stop with the baby talk. Emma's gonna think her name is 'Emmie-Wemmie.'

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? It's cute!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's cute when she's two. When she's sixteen and filling out college applications, 'Emmie-Wemmie' is gonna be a problem.

jokes.text: What are you, a child? Yes!
jokes.setup: Ross asks Joey if he's a child for wanting what he can't have
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately and proudly confirms he is indeed a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect quick exchange - Joey's immediate enthusiastic admission is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, you're like a child. You want what you can't have.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I am a child!

jokes.text: Just think of him as a really, really, really horny Ben.
jokes.setup: Ross compares Joey to his son Ben in childishness
jokes.punchline: Adds the deeply uncomfortable sexual element to the comparison
jokes.explanation: Appropriately uncomfortable comparison, though the setup is a bit forced

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, Joey, sometimes I look at you and I see Ben. You're both equally mature, you both think with your stomachs, and you both have this inability to think about consequences.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, what are you saying?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm just saying you're like a child. And the really uncomfortable part? I'm attracted to you.

jokes.text: Take me home.
jokes.setup: Ross asks Chandler to focus sexual energy on someone else
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan response suggesting Ross redirect to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler timing and delivery - economy makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You need to focus your sexual energy on someone else.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, if you insist.

jokes.text: Do you want a piece of this?
jokes.setup: Monica is aggressively preparing party food
jokes.punchline: Her confrontational attitude while doing domestic tasks
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica intensity applied to party prep, visual element inferred

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? I don't care if you like it or not. These appetizers are PERFECT, and if you can't appreciate that, then you can just leave!

jokes.text: No. No, to test his neck strength.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks if he's setting traps to kill Bob
jokes.punchline: Mike's sarcastic response about testing the rat's fitness
jokes.explanation: Good dry sarcasm with absurd alternative explanation for rat traps

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: No, I'm not killing Bob. I'm just testing his fitness level.

jokes.text: I thought we were gonna capture him and set him free in the countryside where he could meet a friendly possum and a wisecracking owl.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what to do with the rat
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's Disney-movie fantasy scenario for rat relocation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - absurd anthropomorphization with specific character types, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We could give it to a nice family. Like, maybe they'll take it to Disneyland and it'll meet Mickey Mouse and they'll become best friends and then maybe Disney will make a movie about it called 'From Rags to Riches: The Mighty Rat' and it'll win an Oscar and I'll cry.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Bob had babies! Bob's a mom! We'll have to think of a new name. I don't know, I kind of like 'Bob' for a girl.
jokes.setup: They discover the rat had babies, revealing Bob is female
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's attachment to the name Bob regardless of gender
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with gender-neutral naming humor

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Her name is still Bob.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: She's a girl. You want to change it?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No! No, Bob is her name. She's always been Bob to me.

jokes.text: Well, maybe it wasn't Bob. Maybe it was a mouse. Susie?
jokes.setup: They think they killed the mother rat
jokes.punchline: Mike's weak attempt to suggest it might have been a different rodent
jokes.explanation: Decent attempt at damage control but not particularly strong

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Maybe it was a different rat?

jokes.text: Who shot J.R.? I have got to get a job.
jokes.setup: Chandler is watching old TV shows during the day
jokes.punchline: References famous Dallas cliffhanger then admits his unemployment problem
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about daytime TV viewing and self-awareness of his situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't believe I'm watching Dallas at 2 in the afternoon. What have I become?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, who shot J.R.? Oh man, I've been unemployed so long I forgot how to keep up with current events. Well, current events from 1980.

jokes.text: Seriously, dude. Three years ago.
jokes.setup: Someone asks 'What's up?'
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out that's an extremely outdated greeting
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about dated slang, very economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What's up? What's up? Could that BE any more outdated? Next you'll be asking me if I want to hang ten or catch some gnarly waves, dude.

jokes.text: Some woman who sounded a lot like Joey called earlier and asked for her daughter, the 'hot nanny.'
jokes.setup: Ross explains why he doesn't trust Joey with the nanny
jokes.punchline: Joey's obvious attempt at deception using terrible disguise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey stupidity - the obviousness of his deception is the joke

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't trust Joey with the nanny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why not? I'm a professional actor!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, you showed up to audition for a doctor wearing a stethoscope you bought at a toy store.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That was method acting!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You also wore a fake mustache that was clearly drawn on with a marker.

jokes.text: You do appear right on the cusp of something.
jokes.setup: Chandler says he could get a job any day
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic agreement about Chandler's job prospects
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic put-down with elegant phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure, any day now. I'm sure companies are just lining up to hire someone who makes jokes during interviews instead of, you know, answering questions.

jokes.text: Because you know once Joey sets his mind on something, more often than not, he's going to have sex with it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Joey's determination with women
jokes.punchline: Chandler reduces all of Joey's goals to sexual conquest
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Joey's single-mindedness

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is, your entire life goal is to sleep with every woman in New York?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, that's not—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because that's what I'm hearing. You want a good job? Sleep with the boss. You want to be a better actor? Sleep with your acting coach. You want world peace? Sleep with world peace.

jokes.text: Nannies like her don't grow on trees. Picturing that tree? I am, yes.
jokes.setup: Ross uses cliché about attractive nannies being rare
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits to immediately visualizing the absurd tree imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of cliché with Chandler's immediate honest visualization

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, it's very rare to find an attractive nanny.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I immediately pictured a tree.

jokes.text: Your thoughts? Plural? All right, fine. I only have one thought.
jokes.setup: Joey says he needs to organize his thoughts
jokes.punchline: Chandler questions the plural, Joey admits he only has one thought
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey stupidity with Chandler's sharp observation - great rhythm

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I need to organize my thoughts.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Thoughts? Plural?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, my thought.

jokes.text: She's forbidden fruit! It's like she's the princess and I'm the stable boy.
jokes.setup: Joey explains his attraction to the unavailable nanny
jokes.punchline: Casts himself in romantic fairy tale terms
jokes.explanation: Good Joey romanticism with fairy tale delusions of grandeur

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She's like... she's like a beautiful princess locked away in a tower. And I'm the knight who's gonna rescue her.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The knight who's married.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, so I'm a knight having an affair. It's still romantic!

jokes.text: Could you set my pillow and blanket on the couch for me, please?
jokes.setup: Ross says she's the most beautiful woman in the world
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defeated request implies he's in trouble at home
jokes.explanation: Good implication that Monica overheard and Chandler's in trouble

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She's the most beautiful woman in the world.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you might want to keep that opinion to yourself when Monica gets here.

jokes.text: This is the stuff great novels are made of. Great novels? Fine. Mediocre porn.
jokes.setup: Joey romanticizes his situation as literary
jokes.punchline: Chandler's correction brings it down to Joey's actual level
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Joey's pretensions with accurate assessment of his actual story

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm like a character in a Hemingway novel—strong, silent, mysterious.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, you're like a character in a menu—appetizer.

jokes.text: Okay then. I'll pass you back to your son. Hey, Mom. No, that's just my secretary.
jokes.setup: Rachel answers Gavin's phone professionally
jokes.punchline: Gavin dismisses her as 'just his secretary' to his mother
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gavin's dismissiveness and creating tension

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Gavin's office.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Hi Mom. No, that's just my secretary.

jokes.text: If you like looking at butts so much, why don't you just go look in a mirror?
jokes.setup: Rachel confronts Gavin about staring at women
jokes.punchline: Calls him a butt/ass in roundabout way
jokes.explanation: Elementary school-level insult, not particularly clever or original

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Gavin, I've noticed you staring at women. That's inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes you do. You're always looking at them like... like you're a donkey and they're carrots.

jokes.text: Thank God you finally said that. I saw you make a note on your pad three hours ago.
jokes.setup: Rachel delivers her prepared insult
jokes.punchline: Gavin reveals he watched her prepare the insult for hours
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation that completely deflates Rachel's moment, very specific detail

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, you know what? You're a jerk!

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Yeah, well I watched you practice that insult for three hours this morning.

jokes.text: I'm going to be very, very nice to you, you mama's boy, starting right now.
jokes.setup: Rachel announces she's taking the high road
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults him while claiming to be nice
jokes.explanation: Good immediate contradiction but fairly predictable structure

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to take the high road and be the bigger person here.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm not going to say anything mean. I'm just going to say that I hope you find someone who's... well, at your level.

jokes.text: I would never miss my secretary's birthday.
jokes.setup: Gavin agrees to come to Rachel's party
jokes.punchline: Emphasizes her subordinate status while accepting invitation
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Gavin's condescension

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: I'll come to your party, Rachel. But only because you asked so nicely. After all, you do work for me.

jokes.text: Right there. That was so fake!
jokes.setup: Monica can't tell when Rachel is being fake, Rachel demonstrates
jokes.punchline: Points out her obvious insincerity to a random person
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of the concept with immediate example

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I love your sweater! It's so cute!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, not at all. It's hideous.

dialogue.speaker: Random Person
dialogue.line: Wow, okay then.

jokes.text: The pile of raw fish, or the tacos? Oh, the tacos. Definitely, the tacos.
jokes.setup: Monica asks about tuna tartare vs mushroom empanadas
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt translation makes the choice obvious
jokes.explanation: Good food humor with Chandler's practical approach to fancy names

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So which do you think - tuna tartare or mushroom empanadas?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, one's raw fish and the other's a fried pocket of sadness, so...

jokes.text: Honey, all those boys had a bet to see if he could knock you over.
jokes.setup: Monica thinks Skippy Lange pushed her because he liked her
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals the cruel reality of childhood bullying
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective puncturing of Monica's romantic delusion

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He pushed me because he liked me.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, he pushed you because you were in front of him.

jokes.text: Wouldn't kick her out of bed. No more vodka for me.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if Monica sees what guys see in the nanny
jokes.punchline: Monica's inappropriate response and immediate self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good drunk Monica moment with immediate regret and self-correction

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, she's very pretty, and she has a great body, and she's always doing these little things that are very—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm a lesbian!

jokes.text: These are my rat babies!
jokes.setup: Rachel gets excited about shoes in a shoebox
jokes.punchline: The box contains baby rats instead
jokes.explanation: Great visual bait-and-switch with perfect Phoebe maternal pride

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, shoes! Finally! I've been waiting all day for these!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Before you open that... I need to tell you something.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I may have borrowed your shoebox last week to help some babies.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Babies? Phoebe, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, but they're really cute and they need a home, so...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Baby rats!

jokes.text: So this is what a stroke feels like.
jokes.setup: Phoebe brought rats to the birthday party
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic physical reaction to the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica control-freak reaction with medical specificity

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: RATS?! At my party?!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God!

jokes.text: Are you comparing my daughter to a rat? No. Seven rats.
jokes.setup: Rachel is offended by the parenting comparison
jokes.punchline: Phoebe makes it worse by multiplying the comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Phoebe makes the insult worse while trying to correct it

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm not like that as a parent!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh honey, you're not a parent. You're like seventeen parents. Seventeen bad ones.

jokes.text: Well, I'm sorry, Rachel, but I'm not like you. Okay? Not everyone can afford help.
jokes.setup: Rachel is upset about Phoebe leaving the party for rats
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggests Rachel is privileged for having help with Emma
jokes.explanation: Good character tension with Phoebe's class-conscious dig at Rachel

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You left the party for rats?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: They needed me! And you know what? You're lucky. You have a nanny to help you with Emma. Some of us have to choose between our social life and our responsibilities.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A nanny that you recommended!

jokes.text: No, where'd you study acting? Molly, people don't study acting.
jokes.setup: Joey talks about acting as a discipline requiring hard work
jokes.punchline: Reveals he never actually studied acting when pressed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey contradiction - pretentious setup immediately deflated by his ignorance

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Acting is a discipline, you know? It requires hard work, dedication, studying the craft...

dialogue.speaker: Molly
dialogue.line: Wait, you studied acting?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, no. But I'm saying it requires it.

jokes.text: No. The leather sticks to my ass.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if Joey was going to have sex on the couch
jokes.punchline: Joey's practical concern about furniture comfort during sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey moment - completely practical concern about impractical furniture for sex

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, yeah, but have you felt this couch? It's not very comfortable.

jokes.text: Look, the truth is, I haven't felt this way about anyone since Rachel. Okay? I didn't think I could ever love again.
jokes.setup: Joey tries to convince Ross he's serious about the nanny
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration about his capacity for love
jokes.explanation: Good Joey romanticism though somewhat predictable for the character

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm serious, Ross. I can love two people at the same time.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, that's not possible.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yes it is! I love you, I love Chandler, I love Phoebe...

jokes.text: It's like my favorite fairy tale come true. The princess, the stable boy and the lesbian.
jokes.setup: Joey discovers the nanny has a girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Updates his fairy tale fantasy to include the unexpected lesbian twist
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that adds unexpected element while maintaining Joey's romantic delusions

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, the nanny has a girlfriend? So she's a lesbian?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh man, that changes everything. Now in my fantasy, it's like... the nanny and her girlfriend are both there, and they're like, 'Joey, we need your help with something,' and I'm like, 'Anything for you ladies.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: This is even better than I thought.

jokes.text: Yeah, not such a problem with rats. No, they're more of a 'love the one you're with' kind of animal.
jokes.setup: Discussion about rat siblings mating
jokes.punchline: Mike's euphemistic description of rats' mating habits
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemistic reference to classic song about lowered standards

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: They're just doing what comes naturally. When a mommy rat and a daddy rat love each other very much, they... express their feelings in a very special way.

jokes.text: Oh, my-- Get off your sister!
jokes.setup: Learning about rat incestuous behavior
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's horrified reaction to witnessing it
jokes.explanation: Good shocked reaction to disturbing animal behavior, well-timed

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Oh my God! They're doing it! They're doing it right now!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait... that's her mother!

jokes.text: That's math I can't even do!
jokes.setup: Calculating exponential rat population growth
jokes.punchline: Phoebe admits the simple math is beyond her abilities
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe mathematical incompetence, though fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, so if two rats have babies, and then those babies have babies, and then those babies have babies...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: How many rats do you end up with?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Because, I mean, I can barely do regular math. Exponential? That's like, a whole other thing.

jokes.text: But to nice families with children and reduced-fat Wheat Thins. Those were Bob's favorites.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to give away the rats
jokes.punchline: Specific memorial to deceased rat Bob's snack preferences
jokes.explanation: Sweet Phoebe moment with specific brand memory showing her attachment

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, I'll give the rats to the lab. But I want a memorial for Bob. Something about how he loved cheese.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A memorial? Phoebe, they're rats.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Not just any rats, Monica. Bob was special. He had refined taste.

jokes.text: I haven't told you about that yet, have I?
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions giving birth to her brother's triplets
jokes.punchline: Realizes she hasn't shared this major life event with Mike
jokes.explanation: Good callback to show history, underplayed reveal of shocking information

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I just realized I never told Mike about the triplets!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I mean, I gave birth to my brother's three children and my boyfriend doesn't even know about it.

jokes.text: No way. You had your party. Now I have mine.
jokes.setup: Rachel offers to help clean up after her party
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals cleaning is her version of a party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - cleaning as personal celebration is spot-on

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'll help you clean up.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, you don't have to do that.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, I want to. It's the least I can do.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, if you insist. Although, honestly, this is kind of my idea of a party.

jokes.text: 9:30?! God! This party was lame.
jokes.setup: Gavin arrives very late to the party
jokes.punchline: Rachel realizes her party ended embarrassingly early
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness about her social life, though not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Gavin! You finally made it!

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Yeah, sorry I'm late. Traffic was crazy. So where is everyone?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well... they left.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Left? When?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Like three hours ago.

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Three hours? But the party just started!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know! And apparently that's all it took for everyone to decide to leave. So... happy early birthday to me, I guess?

jokes.text: Let me explain how birthday parties usually work. There are presents and a cake, perhaps a fourth or fifth person.
jokes.setup: Rachel questions why Gavin brought a present
jokes.punchline: Gavin's sarcastic explanation of basic party elements she's missing
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic observation about her pathetic party attendance

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why did you bring a present?

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Well, it's a party. People bring presents to parties. And cake. There's usually cake. And music. You know, basic party stuff.

jokes.text: Yes. I feel that you're a little annoying.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if Gavin has feelings for her
jokes.punchline: He confirms he has feelings, but they're negative
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on double meaning of 'having feelings'

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Do you have feelings for me?

dialogue.speaker: Gavin
dialogue.line: Yeah, I do. But they're negative feelings.

jokes.text: Rat baby! Rat baby! Rat baby! Rat baby! Maybe that's him.
jokes.setup: Phoebe looking for missing rat baby
jokes.punchline: Sound effect suggests baby rat responded to her calling
jokes.explanation: Cute ending with Phoebe's maternal calling and implied rat response

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Mr. Whiskers? Mr. Whiskers, baby? Where are you?

dialogue.speaker: Sound Effect
dialogue.line: *squeaking sound*

tmdb_overview: Gavin gives Rachel a very interesting birthday gift while Ross begs Joey to stay away from Emma's hot new nanny. Meanwhile, Mike meets and then accidentally murders Phoebe's unusual pet.
insight: Season 9 dips slightly with rat-plot setup jokes landing just under elite threshold at 79.5.
